title: Record a canonical transaction
preconditions: The target owned account exists and is active; input follows a discoverable contract; provider parsing occurred outside LEDGER; the request includes an idempotency identity and privacy-safe initial evidence.
postconditions: Exactly one canonical transaction and one logical initial evidence effect exist; provider payloads are absent; same replay returns the original outcome.
main_flow: 1. The caller supplies exact canonical transaction facts plus a generic evidence kind, opaque external reference, privacy-safe metadata, and idempotency identity. 2. Tally validates financial semantics, account state, evidence boundary, and attribution references. 3. Tally checks logical evidence and request replay before creating anything. 4. Tally atomically records at most one canonical transaction and links the initial evidence without embedding provider payloads. 5. Tally returns the transaction, evidence identity, current reconciliation state, and contract version. 6. A later query round-trips every accepted fact and link exactly.
alt_flows: 1a. Transaction, evidence, attribution, or idempotency input is invalid -> reject with structured errors
2a. Account is absent or archived -> reject without mutation
2b. Evidence contains provider payload or prohibited sensitive identifiers -> reject without retention
3a. The request or evidence already exists identically -> return the existing result with no duplicate effect
3b. The key or evidence identity conflicts with existing facts or another active transaction -> return a stable conflict and preserve the original
4a. Storage fails or is interrupted -> commit transaction plus evidence link together or leave neither
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner or external orchestrator has one provider-neutral financial event to record from an agent capture, statement row, receipt, external document, or owner assertion.","status":"draft"}
